title: P1 review fixes: require iss/aud, and prove alg-substitution is rejected
action:
Two P1 findings from independent review session review-cf-access-jwt-auth-20260722 must be fixed:

(1) SECURITY P1: in verify_with_key (src/web/cf_access.rs), Validation::new(Algorithm::RS256) only seeds required_spec_claims with "exp" by default -- iss and aud are validated ONLY IF PRESENT, not required. A Cloudflare-signed token that happens to omit aud and/or iss would currently be ACCEPTED. Fix: after building `validation`, call `validation.set_required_spec_claims(&["exp", "iss", "aud"]);` so a token missing any of the three is rejected outright, matching CONTEXT.md's explicit safety condition that aud must actually be checked. Add regression tests: a validly-signed token with the aud claim entirely omitted -> Err; a validly-signed token with the iss claim entirely omitted -> Err.

(2) TEST-COVERAGE P1: no test proves algorithm-substitution (the classic JWT auth-bypass class) is rejected, even though RS256 is pinned via Validation::new(Algorithm::RS256) (independently verified by the security reviewer to be enforced by the jsonwebtoken crate's decode path). Add two regression tests: (a) a token with header alg="none" (unsigned) is rejected; (b) an HS256 token signed using the RSA public key's components as an HMAC secret (the classic RS256->HS256 confusion attack) is rejected. These pin the current safe default so a future refactor that ever widens the accepted algorithm set gets caught by CI.

While in this function, also fix a P2 code-quality finding on the same lines: certs_url() trims a trailing slash from team_domain (line ~432) but set_issuer(&[team_domain]) uses the raw untrimmed value -- if an operator configures team_domain with a trailing slash, the JWKS fetch still works but iss now never matches, silently rejecting every single token with no distinguishing error. Fix: normalize (trim trailing '/') team_domain once in CfAccessVerifier::new so both certs_url() and the issuer check use the same normalized value consistently.

truths: A validly-signed token missing the aud claim is rejected
A validly-signed token missing the iss claim is rejected
A token with alg=none is rejected
An HS256 token signed with the RSA public key's components as the HMAC secret is rejected
A team_domain configured with a trailing slash still matches the iss claim of tokens issued for that team (both certs_url and issuer check use the same normalized value)
All pre-existing tests still pass unmodified in behavior (only the fixed normalization/validation changes outcomes, and only for the previously-broken cases)

artifacts.substantive: set_required_spec_claims added; team_domain normalized once at construction; 4 new regression tests (aud-omitted, iss-omitted, alg=none, HS256 confusion)

prohibitions: Do not touch src/web/auth.rs or src/web/mod.rs in this cell -- scope is cf_access.rs only
Do not weaken any existing validation (exp/nbf/RS256-pinning must remain exactly as strict as before)
Do not change the public verify()/CfAccessVerifier::new() signatures

verify: cargo build --quiet 2>&1 | tail -60 && cargo test --quiet cf_access 2>&1 | tail -60 && cargo fmt --all --check 2>&1 | tail -30 && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tail -60

trace.outcome: Require iss/aud, normalize team_domain, add alg-substitution regression tests
trace.verification_evidence: {"red_failure_evidence":"Pre-fix RED: with set_required_spec_claims removed, `cargo test cf_access` shows missing_aud_claim_is_rejected and missing_iss_claim_is_rejected FAILED (assertion failed: verify_with_key(...).is_err()) — tokens omitting aud/iss were accepted; test result FAILED 18 passed 2 failed. Separately, with team_domain normalization removed, trailing_slash_team_domain_still_matches_iss FAILED (Err Invalid(\"InvalidIssuer\")); FAILED 19 passed 1 failed.","green_success_evidence":"Post-fix GREEN, full four-part verify: cargo build --quiet clean; cargo test --quiet cf_access = 20 passed 0 failed 0 ignored; cargo fmt --all --check clean no diff; cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings Finished 0 warnings.","notes":"Fixes in src/web/cf_access.rs: (1) validation.set_required_spec_claims([exp,iss,aud]) so a token omitting iss or aud is rejected; (2) team_domain trimmed of trailing / once in CfAccessVerifier::new so certs_url() and the issuer check agree. Added regression tests: missing_aud, missing_iss, alg=none, HS256 key-confusion, trailing-slash iss match. No existing validation weakened."}
